root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively shut (shutdown in running-config) which brought the R2 interface admin_state to down; this removed carrier on the link and caused R1 Serial0/1/0 to go to lower-layer-down while its admin_state remained up. Remediation required: yes (undo shutdown on R2 if the interface should be active).
evidence: OSPF on both routers (get_ospf): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" on R1 and R2 (no neighbors listed)

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively shut (shutdown in running-config) which brought the R2 interface admin_state to down; this removed carrier on the link and caused R1 Serial0/1/0 to go to lower-layer-down while its admin_state remained up. Remediation required: yes (undo shutdown on R2 if the interface should be active).
evidence: OSPF on both routers (get_ospf): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" on R1 and R2 (no neighbors listed)

data.intent_description: Investigate two interface operational state changes: R1 Serial0/1/0 moved from up -> lower-layer-down while admin state remains up; R2 Serial0/1/0 moved from up -> down with admin state changed to down. Determine root cause and whether remediation is required.

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively shut (shutdown in running-config) which brought the R2 interface admin_state to down; this removed carrier on the link and caused R1 Serial0/1/0 to go to lower-layer-down while its admin_state remained up. Remediation required: yes (undo shutdown on R2 if the interface should be active).
evidence: OSPF on both routers (get_ospf): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" on R1 and R2 (no neighbors listed)

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively shut (admin down) which removed carrier and caused R1 Serial0/1/0 to go lower-layer-down; goal is to bring R2 Serial0/1/0 admin up so the link and OSPF adjacency are restored.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut (shutdown in running-config) causing the link to R1 to lose carrier and OSPF adjacency.
plan.fix_summary: Undo the administrative shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (set admin up) to restore link carrier and OSPF neighbors.

evidence: OSPF on both routers (get_ospf): Serial0/1/0 interface_state = "Down" on R1 and R2 (no neighbors listed)

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shut (shutdown in running-config) causing the link to R1 to lose carrier and OSPF adjacency.
plan.fix_summary: Undo the administrative shutdown on R2 Serial0/1/0 (set admin up) to restore link carrier and OSPF neighbors.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively down; R1 Serial0/1/0 showed admin up but oper lower-layer-down. The OSPF interface on both routers was Down.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- R2 had a "shutdown" on Serial0/1/0 causing loss of carrier and the OSPF adjacency to drop.

Changes made
- Enabled the interface on R2 (undo shutdown / set admin up on Serial0/1/0).

Verification
- Passed: R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up and R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up oper=up; OSPF adjacency on the link is now Full; R2 running-config no longer contains "shutdown".
- Remaining: RESTCONF/OSPF fetch for R3 failed with 401 Unauthorized (monitoring/visibility issue) — unrelated to the link fix and should be addressed separately.

Verification

- Remaining: RESTCONF/OSPF fetch for R3 failed with 401 Unauthorized (monitoring/visibility issue) — unrelated to the link fix and should be addressed separately.
